skill.name: angular-ui-patterns
skill.description: Modern Angular UI guidance for loading states, error handling, progressive disclosure, forms, dialogs, and data display patterns.
skill.summary: Apply practical Angular UI patterns for resilient async component states.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network links, external command patterns, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators. AI review found documentation-only Angular examples and reference URLs, with no executable scripts, runtime network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Activity
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs are reference links to Angular documentation, Angular Material, and ng-spartan. They do not perform runtime requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are metadata and markdown references only. No executable fetch, request, webhook, or data transfer behavior is present in those locations.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks and fenced blocks contain Angular, TypeScript, HTML, and prose examples. They are instructional snippets, not shell, Ruby, or process execution code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows markdown formatting and Angular UI examples. No command runner, shell invocation, subprocess API, or user-controlled execution path appears in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is a Substring False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high-severity cryptography detections are caused by ordinary words such as SKILL.md and description. No MD5, DES, SHA1, or cryptographic operation is recommended or executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain descriptive text or field names, not cryptographic APIs. The scanner appears to match algorithm names inside unrelated words.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Is UI Example Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations describe Angular UI anti-patterns, form button markup, and validation helpers. They do not inspect the host, enumerate files, or collect system details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are ordinary UI documentation and Angular form logic. No commands such as uname, whoami, hostname, env, or filesystem enumeration appear in context.

content.value_statement: Angular applications often handle loading, empty, error, and submission states inconsistently. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear patterns for resilient component behavior.
actual_capabilities: Chooses between skeletons, spinners, empty states, and existing data displays.
Defines error handling hierarchy for fields, toasts, banners, and full-screen errors.
Shows Angular control flow patterns using @if, @for, @empty, and @defer.
Guides form validation, submit disabling, and async submission feedback.
Provides dialog confirmation and modal lifecycle patterns.
Lists UI state checklist items for loading, errors, data, and accessibility.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install Angular packages.
It assumes modern Angular syntax with signals and control flow blocks.
It does not provide complete production components or styling systems.
It references patterns, but final accessibility testing remains project-specific.

use_cases.title: Standardize Async Components
use_cases.description: Apply consistent loading, empty, and error states across Angular lists, cards, forms, and detail pages.

use_cases.title: Review UI State Coverage
use_cases.description: Check whether a component handles retries, stale data, disabled actions, and visible failure states.

use_cases.title: Guide AI Code Generation
use_cases.description: Give Claude, Codex, or Claude Code concrete Angular patterns before generating or refactoring UI components.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Loading States
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the angular-ui-patterns skill to add loading, empty, and error states to this Angular list component.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Angular component with the angular-ui-patterns skill and make every async failure visible to the user.

prompt_templates.title: Design Deferred Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the angular-ui-patterns skill to decide which page sections should load immediately and which should use @defer.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the angular-ui-patterns checklist to this create, edit, and delete workflow. Identify missing states and propose fixes.

output_examples.input: A table reloads and replaces existing rows with a spinner.
output_examples.output: The response recommends keeping existing rows visible during refresh and showing a subtle progress indicator instead.

output_examples.input: A submit button can be clicked repeatedly while saving.
output_examples.output: The response recommends disabling the button during submission and showing clear in-button progress feedback.

output_examples.input: A comments section slows the first render of an article page.
output_examples.output: The response recommends loading critical content first and deferring comments with placeholder, loading, and error states.

best_practices: Show loading indicators only when no useful data is available.
Surface every async error through visible, recoverable UI feedback.
Disable repeated actions while async work is in progress.
anti_patterns: Replacing existing data with a full spinner during background refreshes.
Catching errors only in logs while users see no failure state.
Creating lists or collections without empty states.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Angular components?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples that guide component generation. Final implementation still depends on the project structure.

faq.question: Which Angular features does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes signals, modern control flow, @defer, async UI states, forms, dialogs, and component feedback.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes reminders for announced loading states, linked errors, and focus management after state changes.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and contains reference links, not executable network code.

faq.question: Is it suitable for older Angular versions?
faq.answer: It is most suitable for modern Angular projects that support signals and new template control flow.

faq.question: How should teams use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Ask the assistant to apply the checklist before generating or reviewing Angular UI components.
